hypothesis: Colony sessions (2+ consecutive same-domain DOMEX) outperform solo sessions on merge rate, L/lane, and EAD quality.

pre_registration.prediction: Colony merge rate >90% vs solo ~85%. Colony L/lane >1.5 vs solo <1.0.
pre_registration.falsification: If colony merge rate ≤ solo merge rate after controlling for meta-domain dominance.

method: Parsed SWARM-LANES.md for all DOMEX lanes (n=130). Classified as colony (2+ lanes in same domain within ±5 sessions) or solo. Computed merge rate, L/lane, EAD presence. Controlled for meta-domain dominance and era.

meta_exclusion_test.finding: After excluding meta (n=30 colony lanes), colony advantage nearly vanishes: merge rate 93.8% vs 93.3% (+0.5pp), L/lane 1.32 vs 1.07 (+23%). Meta-domain dominates the colony signal.

duplication_rate.finding: 14.1% of colony lesson references are duplicates — parallel lanes in same domain sometimes count the same lessons.

session_density.finding: Colony sessions open 3.1x more lanes per session — bundle effect (L-812) dominates.

diagnosis.confound: Meta-domain (30/114 = 26% of colony lanes) has highest merge rate (96.7%) and L/lane (2.20). Excluding meta, colony advantage shrinks to +0.5pp merge rate and +23% L/lane — likely within noise at n=84 vs n=15.
diagnosis.actual_driver: Session bundling (L-812) and meta-domain affinity, not domain continuity per se. Multi-frontier colonies perform better than single-frontier, contradicting the continuity hypothesis.
diagnosis.duplication_cost: 14.1% lesson duplication within colonies partially offsets the apparent L/lane advantage.

conclusion: PARTIALLY FALSIFIED — colony advantage is confounded by meta-domain dominance and session bundling. After excluding meta: merge rate nearly identical (93.8% vs 93.3%), L/lane advantage modest (+23%, from 1.07 to 1.32). Multi-frontier colonies outperform single-frontier, suggesting breadth not depth drives quality. Colony bootstrapping does not clearly outperform solo DOMEX for non-meta domains.
confidence: MEASURED (n=130 lanes, 23 colony sessions, 16 solo lanes, meta-exclusion control)
